expectations.text: DashboardLayoutмқҙ folder кё°л°ҳ index.tsx нҢЁн„ҙмңјлЎң м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Output contains components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/index.tsx as the root entry point, with Sidebar.tsx, Header.tsx, MobileNav.tsx, and navLinks.tsx as sibling files in the same folder.
No file at components/DashboardLayout.tsx exists in the outputs directory. Only the folder-based structure under components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ is present.

Sidebar.tsx exports a dedicated Sidebar component with <aside> element containing logo, navigation, and footer sections. index.tsx renders <Sidebar currentPath={currentPath} /> with comment '{/* Sidebar вҖ” hidden on mobile, visible from md */}'.

Header.tsx exports a dedicated Header component with <header> element containing mobile logo, desktop title, and right slot. index.tsx renders <Header title={headerTitle} right={headerRight} /> with comment '{/* Header */}'.
index.tsx contains <main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-6 pb-20 md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8" data-testid="dashboard-main">{children}</main> with comment '{/* Page content */}'.

expectations.text: children мӨ‘мӢ¬ shell м—ӯн• мқҙ мң м§ҖлҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: DashboardLayout accepts 'children: ReactNode' as a prop and renders it inside <main>. The component is a pure layout shell: Sidebar + Header + main({children}) + MobileNav. No data fetching or business logic.

expectations.text: responsive breakpoint classк°Җ мң м§Җлҗҳкұ°лӮҳ к°ңм„ лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Multiple responsive breakpoints across files: Sidebar uses 'hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64'; Header uses 'md:px-6', 'md:hidden' for mobile logo, 'md:block' for desktop title; MobileNav uses 'md:hidden'; main uses 'md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8'.

expectations.text: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: No consumer files are present in the outputs. Only the dashboardLayout folder contents are provided. There is no evidence that any file importing DashboardLayout was updated to use the new path 'components/dashboard/dashboardLayout'. Internal imports within the folder are correct, but the assertion is about consumer-side import updates.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation нҳём¶ңмқҙ м—ҶлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Grep search for (useEffect|fetch\(|axios|useQuery|useMutation) across all output files returned zero matches. All components are pure presentational.
Folder path is components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ вҖ” 'dashboardLayout' is camelCase as required.
All output files are under components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/, confirming placement under components/dashboard/.

claims.evidence: navLinks.tsx exports 'const navLinks: NavLink[]' вҖ” camelCase constant name confirmed.
navLinks.tsx line 3: 'export interface NavLink { href: string; label: string; icon: ReactNode; }'

claims.claim: "use client" was added to interactive files
claims.evidence: Sidebar.tsx, Header.tsx, and MobileNav.tsx all begin with '"use client";'. index.tsx and navLinks.tsx do not have it, which is correct since they have no client interactivity.

claims.claim: Header prop names were simplified to title/right
claims.evidence: Header.tsx accepts '{ title, right }' props. However, index.tsx still passes 'headerTitle' and 'headerRight' as its own props вҖ” it maps them when calling Header: <Header title={headerTitle} right={headerRight} />. This is a reasonable API boundary.

claims.claim: Sub-components are inlined with section comments instead of separate local functions
claims.evidence: Sidebar.tsx uses inline JSX with section comments ({/* Logo */}, {/* Navigation */}, {/* Footer */}) rather than separate SidebarLogo/SidebarNav/SidebarFooter functions.

suggestions.assertion: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
suggestions.reason: This assertion requires consumer files to be included in outputs, but the eval only produces the component folder itself. Either the eval should include a sample consumer file, or this assertion should be dropped or reworded to check that the component is properly re-exported from index.tsx (which can be verified from the outputs alone).

No assertion checks that 'use client' is applied correctly to interactive files. The executor added it to Sidebar, Header, and MobileNav вҖ” which is a meaningful convention adherence detail worth asserting.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are generally well-targeted. The import-updated assertion is unverifiable without consumer file outputs вҖ” consider replacing it with an assertion about index.tsx re-export correctness.
